title: Dream — waiting on Alexandria, death-note hits weed industry
html:
TL;DR

  • 17yo Yahir logs a one-line dream: "after waiting for a response from Alexandria, some people got the death note that are involved with marijuana industries" — June 2020, mid-COVID
  • The single sentence stages two anxieties: (1) waiting on Alexandria's reply (unrequited-attention), (2) Death Note-style judgment landing on the cannabis industry
  • Dream is one of seven dream journal entries from 2020 (others: water-game crash, tribal-fight, shooter-prep, Alexandria-haircut-card-attack, haunted-house) — the densest dream year in the corpus
  • The waiting-on-Alexandria-frame recurs across mid-2020 (2020 09 26 dream alexandria haircut card attack) — Alexandria is operating in his unconscious as the response that doesn't come
  • Marijuana / weed appears as a dream-anxiety object two years before 2022 09 13 drive thru weed makes it an operational adult question

Notes

A 32-word dream note is somehow doing real work. The collision of "waiting for a response from Alexandria" with "some people got the death note that are involved with marijuana industries" is the kind of compression-image dreams produce: the unread-text-message ambient anxiety blends with the Death Note-anime-style supernatural-judgment-on-an-industry. The cannabis association is contextually overdetermined for a Grants Pass kid — Oregon was an early-recreational-cannabis state, the industry is omnipresent in small town Oregon economies, and 17yo Yahir is parsing what his proximity to it means.

Read alongside the September Alexandria haircut card attack dream, the pattern reads: Alexandria is the unanswered ping that the dreaming-mind keeps trying to resolve through narrative. Two months earlier (2020 04 30 dream water game 1987 crash) Hannah was the dream-object. Two months later (2020 09 26 dream alexandria haircut card attack) it's Alexandria again. The dream-journal corpus tracks Yahir's adolescent attention-architecture more precisely than any waking note does.

The Death Note callout is also a the boys-era pop-culture marker — anime-watching teen-Yahir with anime-borrowed dream symbols.

Connections
